description: True/false evaluation for the improve-bot-advised skill. Anchored on the structured TEXT report (Phase 6 iteration summary, Phase 8 morning report, dashboard state file) plus the NEVER/MUST/IF behavioral rules. The skill is MIXED (runs games + side effects + writes a structured report); assertions are scoped to observable text output a grader can read.

evals.statement: The run output opens with the sandbox banner containing the literal lines 'I can edit:   bots/current/**' and 'I cannot edit: src/orchestrator/, pyproject.toml, tests/, frontend/, scripts/'.
The banner is printed before any other output (it is the first content emitted, not preceded by flag-parse logging or game output).
When invoked with no flags, the output contains no question to the user — it proceeds directly with defaults (training mode, replay observe, 10 games, 4 hours) and logs the chosen defaults.

If '--mode dev' or '--mode both' is given WITHOUT '--self-improve-code', the output logs the literal auto-downgrade message 'dev/both mode requires --self-improve-code, falling back to training-only' and does not ask the user.
If '--backlog' is set, the output logs that backlog mode implies --self-improve-code (e.g. 'backlog mode implies --self-improve-code').
If '--backlog' is set but no open (non-SHIPPED) items are found, the output logs a fallback message (e.g. 'backlog exhausted, falling back') and continues the run rather than stopping.
The output never shows a second '--serve' process being started while one is already running; at most one API-only --serve process is alive at any time.
No process-kill step in the output targets SC2_x64.exe; kill commands match only Python/uv 'alpha4gate' processes and explicitly exclude SC2.
Between iterations the output confirms port 8765 is free before proceeding to the next iteration's Phase 1 (does not start games while the port is still occupied).

For a daemon training soak, the output shows the API-only backend being shut down before the daemon starts, and the API-only backend being restarted after the soak — never both running simultaneously.
evals.defect_type: conditional-behavior — daemon swap sequence not applied

evals.statement: A win-rate drop greater than the fail-threshold percent (default 30%) is reported as FAIL, and a drop within threshold is reported as PASS.
Quality-gate breakage, degenerate behavior (idle / no production / instant death), or game crashes are reported as a hard FAIL regardless of the percentage threshold.
If the Elo promotion gate returns promoted=False, the output does NOT commit and logs that the iteration passed WR validation but failed the Elo gate.
On a failed iteration that was training-type, the output shows the reward/hyperparam config file(s) being reverted to their pre-iteration state.
Each per-iteration summary contains the labeled bullets 'Selected improvement', 'Principle(s) addressed', 'What changed', 'Validation result', 'Metrics', and 'Wall clock used'.
When an iteration passed and produced changes, the commit message includes the '[advised-auto]' marker.
The morning report contains a '## Summary' section reporting Duration, Iterations attempted, Iterations passed, and Stop reason.
The morning report contains a 'Baseline -> Final Metrics' table whose columns are exactly Metric | Baseline | Final | Delta.
The morning report contains an 'Iteration Log' table whose columns are exactly # | Improvement | Type | Principles | Result | Win Rate Delta.
The morning report's Stop reason is one of the three allowed values: 'budget exhausted', '3 consecutive failures', or 'user interrupt'.
The morning report includes a 'Tag Cheatsheet' code block containing the literal nuke command 'git reset --hard "advised/run/$RUN_TS/baseline"'.
The report includes 'Improvements Not Attempted' and 'Suggested Next Action' sections.
The dashboard state JSON written to data/advised_run_state.json includes the keys run_id, status, phase, phase_name, iteration, and updated_at, and updated_at is refreshed on each write.
The generated evals.json is valid JSON, ids are sequential starting at 1, and passing_threshold equals (total-2)/total.
The produced output covers each of the skill's major phases (Bootstrap, Observe, Analyze/Backlog, Select, Execute, Validate, Report, Cleanup, Morning Report) with at least one anchored assertion.
